working: Constant Contact open rates are exceptional: 52.09% average across 100 campaigns vs the ~25-35% health/nonprofit benchmark; the newest send 'Alumni eNews: Final Reminder - June 2026' (6/3) still opened 41.42%.
The biggest traffic lift came from 'Staff-Client eNews - May 2026' (5/19), +66.5% above baseline (17 -> 28.3 daily sessions) -- the large ~9,700-send Staff-Client list moves the needle most.

SEO is climbing: GSC clicks jumped 2 -> 23, impressions 1,681 -> 4,536, pages with data 54 -> 73, and impression-weighted avg position improved 21.36 -> 19.15 vs the 6/3 run; Organic Search holds as the #2 channel at 824 sessions (35.7%).
'Alumni eNews - May 2026' (5/2) had the strongest engagement per open at 1.79% click / 3.77% click-to-open.
not_working: UTM leakage is total: all 12 newsletter sends show email_sessions_3d=0 while posting direct_sessions_3d of 27-76, so newsletter links carry no UTM tags and GA4 misattributes every email visit to Direct -- which is why Direct sits at 1,316 sessions (57.0%).
Organic Social is negligible at 37 sessions (1.6%), far below the 5% threshold.
Click-through is weak despite strong opens: avg click rate 2.07% and most sends land under 1% (Final Reminder June 0.69%, Alumni June 1.08%), with click-to-open near 1.7%.
Several recent sends had negative lift: 'Alumni eNews - May 2026' -50.2%, 'Staff-Client eNews - April 2026' -15.7%, 'Alumni eNews - June 2026' -9.8%.
do_next: Add utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email (plus utm_campaign) to every newsletter link so GA4 credits Email -- this should reclaim a large share of the 57% Direct bucket and finally let us measure newsletter ROI.
Lean into the Staff-Client list on mid-week sends: the +66.5% lift came from the ~9.7k Staff-Client send on Tue 5/19, so prioritize that list and cadence over the small ~1k Alumni list.
Convert opens to clicks: 52% opens aren't translating (CTOR ~1.7%) -- tighten subject-to-CTA alignment, use one clear primary CTA, and repromote the Alumni-May themes that hit 3.77% CTOR.
Keep pressing SEO: clicks and impressions both rose this run and avg position improved to 19.15 -- continue the location/keyword page work now surfacing 73 pages in GSC.
summary: Email engagement is strong on opens (52% avg, well above benchmark) and the large Staff-Client list drives real traffic lift, but missing UTM tags hide all newsletter sessions inside the 57% Direct bucket; SEO is quietly improving (GSC clicks 2->23, position 21.36->19.15). The single highest-value fix is adding UTM tags to newsletter links.
